data.title: The last-mile delivery market valued at US$166.45 billion in 2024, projected to reach US$311.31 billion by 2031, growing at 9.62% CAGR.
data.description: In October 2025, it was reported that the worldwide last-mile delivery sector had a value of approximately US$166.45 billion in 2024. Projections indicate that this market is expected to increase to around US$311.31 billion by 2031, with a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 9.62%. The growth of this industry is primarily driven by the expansion of e-commerce, increased reliance on swift delivery services, and other factors.

Russian President Vladimir Putin joked he would ‘no longer fly drones over Denmark’, after incidents last week shut airports. Moscow denies responsibility, calling Western concern over drone incursions ‘hysteria’. Poland and NATO allies shot down Russian drones in September.

data.title: Protolabs appoints Marc Kermisch as new Chief Technology and AI Officer, effective Oct. 13, 2025, succeeding Oleg Ryaboy.
data.description: In Minneapolis, today, digital manufacturing leader Protolabs (NYSE: PRLB) revealed Marc Kermisch as the new Chief Technology and AI Officer, taking on the role from Oct. 13, 2025. Kermisch will oversee Protolabs' worldwide technology sector following the departure of former CTO, Oleg Ryaboy. CEO Suresh Krishna expressed gratitude to Ryaboy for his contributions over the past 3 years.

data.title: XRP up 1.5% as it approaches US ETF deadline, consolidating near $3 with 200-day EMA at $2.62 and 20/50 EMA at $2.92-2.93.
data.description: XRP, a cryptocurrency, saw a 1.5% increase on Friday, staying within a tightening triangle as investors considered Ripple's leadership changes, Japan's push towards institutional lending, and upcoming U.S. ETF deadlines. The current XRP price sits at $3.02, where September's volatility tested trendline resistance and ascending support. The token is compressed in a symmetrical triangle on the daily chart, with the 200-day EMA at $2.62 and the 20/50 EMA cluster at $2.92-$2.93 as significant turning points. A break above $3.15 would indicate a bullish trend, potentially leading to higher levels. For more details, visit Benzinga.com.
US House Speaker Mike Johnson ends remarks.

data.title: Bitcoin faces potential squeeze with $88B record open interest, sparking concerns of a imminent "liquidity flush" in 2 weeks.
data.description: The open interest for Bitcoin futures saw a significant increase, reaching the highest levels on record. This surge is indicating a potential "liquidity flush" expected to occur within the next two weeks. It suggests a growing interest and participation in Bitcoin futures trading, with market activity projected to pick up notably in the near future.

data.title: Envision Energy partners for 1GW Mirny Wind Project in Kazakhstan, country's first major renewable energy initiative.
data.description: On October 3, 2025, in Astana, Kazakhstan, Envision Energy, a prominent company in green technology, revealed a significant partnership in Kazakhstan's Mirny Wind Farm. This marks a groundbreaking venture as the country's primary renewable energy initiative, boasting a massive capacity of 1 GW. The collaboration was solidified through the signing of a Letter of Awrad (LOA), signifying a crucial step in promoting sustainable energy practices in the region.

The United Nations World Food Programme said on Friday it will slash the number of people it supports with emergency food aid in Somalia by two-thirds to just 350,000 next month, down from 1.1 million in August, because of a funding shortfall.
